{"file_path":"/home/runner/work/Kody-Engine-Tester/Kody-Engine-Tester/.kody/tasks/2656-260418-100644/stateless-leaping-origami.md","content":"# Plan: Add User Payload Collection with PBKDF2 Password Hash Field

## Context

The codebase has a dual auth system — `UserStore` (SHA-256, in-memory) coexists with `AuthService` (PBKDF2, JWT) — causing inconsistent user representation and password storage. This task introduces a unified `User` Payload collection that uses PBKDF2 consistently (matching AuthService) as the authoritative credential store. The existing `users` Payload collection already has `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, and `role` fields (confirmed from a pre-existing type error in `tests/helpers/seedUser.ts`); this task adds `passwordHash` and the hashing hook.

## Existing Patterns Found

- **Payload collection pattern** (`src/collections/*.ts`): Collections use `CollectionConfig` with `fields`, `hooks`, `access`, and `upload`. Hooks are typed with `H` from `payload`.… [+11478 chars]

{"plan":"# Plan: Add User Payload Collection with PBKDF2 Password Hash Field

## Context

The codebase has a dual auth system — `UserStore` (SHA-256, in-memory) coexists with `AuthService` (PBKDF2, JWT) — causing inconsistent user representation and password storage. This task introduces a unified `User` Payload collection that uses PBKDF2 consistently (matching AuthService) as the authoritative credential store. The existing `users` Payload collection already has `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, and `role` fields (confirmed from a pre-existing type error in `tests/helpers/seedUser.ts`); this task adds `passwordHash` and the hashing hook.
